name: Otto Hardwick
abstract: Otto James "Toby" Hardwicke (May 31, 1904 – August 5, 1970) was an American saxophone player associated with Duke Ellington.

works.t: Black, Brown and Beige: Work Song

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: And Otto Hardwick played the -- the release, and I went back to the first eight bars. That was the basic tune of 'Sophisticated Lady'. And I got the terrific check of $15.00 for -- for writing 'Sophisticated Lady'

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: When I joined Duke Ellington in the Cotton Club... we had Otto Hardwick, Hiwork, saxophone...

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I'll tell you during that time where I was doing a lot of writing, on a little small label called HRS, and let's see, who did we have, Harry Carney, Otto Hardwick, Lawrence Brown, Shelley Manne, Billy Taylor the bass player.

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I was listening recently to a whole old Duke Ellington 1940s band, the reeds section of Hodges, Otto Hardwick, Ben Webster, Harry Carney, Barney Bigard, -- to me, that was one of the greatest reed sections in the world.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: when I joined the band, Otto Hardwick, he wouldn't show up sometimes for two and three nights and I always wondered what is he doing... I decided I'm going to hang out with him and find out what he's doing

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: It's the fact that he has literally used their material, you see? And the music is published with their names on it... It says, sophisticated lady, Otto Hardwick and Duke Ellington.
